calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #7, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Mar-10-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Nancy Levin for Trustee, Debra Dolch.

Ms. Levin addressed the notes, stating that she received communication that two of them got married and their names have changed so she added the AKA, but that might be a problem, so she changed it back. Ms. Levin further stated that the fees did not change, that she didn't charge her rate because she billed at the hourly rate of her associate, that she does remember paying $29.00 for a certified copy of an order, that she doesn't mind if the court reduces the efiling fee, that she charged paralegal rate as oppose to associate's rate which saved the Trust almost $700.00. In addition, Ms. Levin stated the beneficiaries have called weekly for their money, that notice was given with regard to the amount and asked that notice on the declaration be waived. The Court received a new proposed order from Ms. Levin in open court.

The Seventh Report and Account of Testamentary Trustee, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: S. Le. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Matter not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #7, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jan-22-2020 Continued To Mar-04-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #6, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Apr-06-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nancy Levin for and with (trustee) Debra Dolch.

The Court stated that we are here on the sixth report and account and request for fees and asked counsel about the governing terms requiring payments for education at an accredited institution. Ms. Levin stated that the school is not accredited by the Department of Education, that it is accredited by a professional association (National Association of Heavy Equipment), that the beneficiary is studying to be a crane operator, that he also took a CPR class and that it is in how you interpret it. The Court stated that it applies to under grad, grad and post graduate study. Ms. Dolch reported that the beneficiary had a difficult time in high school, that he tried community and junior college and then came forward with the plan to be a heavy equipment operator. Ms. Levin stated that it is under the National Commission of Crane Operators. The Court stated that it does not doubt that it is creditable, but can the court approve it or just confirm that it has been done and that no one has objected. Ms. Dolch stated that maybe they need a separate petition and that she is finding these kinds of requests with this generation. The Court stated that the trust is straightforward. Ms. Dolch stated that there are 22 beneficiaries and that the older grandchildren went to traditional schools. The Court questioned travel and clothing as reasonable education expenses. Ms. Dolch clarified that there was specific required clothing to wear during school training and that the travel expense was necessary to get to the school. The Court stated that it does not doubt the good intentions but that the language of the trust is fairly specific. Ms. Levin stated that this particular beneficiary lived at home so did not incur living expenses. The Court stated that maybe the trust language should have been broader but is says what it says and directed the questioning to attorney's fees. Ms. Levin stated that she has the invoices, that in the past they have always done it this way, that in the last report the probate examiner asked for time sheets and said that if they did not get them the fees would be allowed but not approved, explained to the court how she spent the time and stated that she could provide the invoices if necessary. The Court stated that it is being asked to look at these things. Ms. Levin stated that she would be happy to provide them and that she could file a detailed declaration. The Court stated that it is headed towards allowing, that the trustee has done what the trustee has done and that some things could be challenged later. The accounting petition was taken under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)
